summary: Verified iteration 2 features: share links (P1), custom cover + set cover from page (P2), rotate, crop, double-page spread, and auto-orientation pages.width/height. 10/10 new backend tests pass and full Playwright e2e of new flows passes. Existing 15/15 regression suite still green.
action_items: (Minor) The data-testid='flipbook' prop on <HTMLFlipBook> is not forwarded to a DOM node by react-pageflip; tests rely on spread-toggle-button / page-indicator instead. If a stable flipbook element is desired, wrap HTMLFlipBook in a div with data-testid='flipbook'.
(Optional) Library card thumbnail does not have its own data-testid for the element; for stronger cover-change assertions, expose data-testid='book-cover-img-' (currently only the card has a testid).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py _reprocess_page reads original image bytes from object storage and writes a new PNG on every rotate/crop. There is no cleanup of the previous storage object - this will accumulate orphaned objects over time. Consider issuing a DELETE on the old path or maintaining a tombstone.

server.py upload_cover does not validate file size or actual image content (only extension). Trusting extension is risky if the storage backend later serves with sniffed content-type. Use Pillow to verify and re-encode covers, similar to page uploads.

server.py rotate_page rotates -degrees with expand=True; that's fine for 90 increments but for arbitrary degrees the resulting black corners are not transparent (PNG saved as RGB). Either save as RGBA or constrain RotateRequest to {90,180,270}.

FlipViewer.jsx changes its HTMLFlipBook 'key' on every spread/aspect resize, which destroys and rebuilds the book and resets current page index. Consider only re-keying on isSpread changes, not w/h drift.
SharedReader.jsx and Reader.jsx both render FlipViewer; FlipViewer fires onPageChange even when unused (Reader supplies one, SharedReader doesn't). Minor - just keep prop optional (already is).
seed_data_creation: Two TEST_ books created (TEST_V2 for v2 suite, UI_V2_Book2 for UI run); both cleaned up via DELETE /api/books/<id> and UI delete confirm.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend selectors for landing/library are 'new-book-button', 'book-title-input', 'create-book-confirm' (not the names from the request: new-flipbook-button / new-book-title-input / confirm-create-book - those would fail). For the editor & reader the testids in the request are correct. To verify the shared reader is read-only, assert the ABSENCE of upload-zone / share-flipbook-button / custom-cover-button / read-flipbook-button rather than the presence of a 'flipbook' element (react-pageflip strips unknown props). PIL was used to fabricate landscape (800x400) and portrait (400x800) images BEFORE launching playwright. Set REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL when invoking pytest.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. One test-side false-positive happened: the data-testid='flipbook' attribute is passed as a prop to HTMLFlipBook but isn't applied to any DOM node, so query_selector returns null. The shared reader is actually rendering correctly (spread-toggle-button and page-indicator are present and functional). Tests were updated to use those reliable selectors.
